tiktok.caption: You're 1.7x more likely to sell your winning stocks than your losing ones — and that habit alone costs you $47,000. Here's why your brain does it.

youtube_short.title: The Investor Habit That Costs You $47,000
title_alternatives: Why You Always Sell the Wrong Stock (It's Your Brain)
The Disposition Effect: The Most Expensive Investing Mistake
youtube_short.description: Studies show investors are 1.7 times more likely to sell their winning stocks than their losing ones — and that one behavioral quirk costs the average retail investor 1.5% per year in returns. It's called the Disposition Effect, and behavioral economists have measured the exact price tag: $47,000 in missing wealth over 30 years. Your brain's loss aversion kicks in and makes a -$1,000 loss feel twice as painful as a +$1,000 gain feels good — so you freeze and hold the loser, hoping it bounces back. The fix is simple: set your stop-loss rule at -15% BEFORE you buy, and stick to it no matter what. #investing #stockmarket #personalfinance #behavioralfinance #wealthbuilding

hook_line: Your brain sells winners and holds losers — costing you $47,000
